_comment: Edit these EXAMPLE rules for your own project. Each rule denies a Bash command whose lowercased text matches `pattern` (Python regex). Keep patterns NARROW — false positives erode trust. Append `# GUARDRAIL_OK` to a command to override. Run /add-guardrail to add a rule from plain English.

rules.name: expensive-llm-model
rules.message: Use the cheap/flash tier for this provider, not the expensive SKU (cost guardrail). EXAMPLE — adapt to your own provider + paid models.

rules.name: protect-critical-instance
rules.message: Refusing to STOP the critical instance. EXAMPLE — replace 'my-prod-box' with your instance name; a stop may be destructive or non-recoverable.

rules.name: no-public-bind
rules.message: Don't bind 0.0.0.0 (no public ingress). Use 127.0.0.1 or a private/VPN address.

rules.name: single-arch-only
rules.message: This host is single-arch (e.g. arm64): no cross-arch binaries. EXAMPLE — adjust for your architecture, or delete this rule.
